error.signature: pip install succeeds for a yanked PyPI package version that was supposed to be removed

verdict.summary: PyPI yank does NOT delete the package. It only hides it from resolution UNLESS the version is pinned exactly in requirements. 'pip install pkg==1.2.3' still installs yanked 1.2.3. Only 'pip install pkg' (unpinned) skips yanked versions.

dead_ends.action: Yank a package version expecting it to be completely unavailable
dead_ends.why_fails: Yanking is a soft-delete. Exact version pins (==) still install yanked versions. Lock files with pinned versions continue to work.

dead_ends.action: Rely on yanking to remove a security vulnerability from circulation
dead_ends.why_fails: All existing requirements.txt files with pinned versions will continue to install the yanked version. You need to publish a new patched version.

workarounds.action: Publish a post-release or patch version with the fix, then yank the bad version
workarounds.how: Upload pkg==1.2.4 with fix, then yank 1.2.3. Users upgrading get 1.2.4. Existing pins still get 1.2.3 (with pip warning).

workarounds.action: Use pip --no-yanked flag or check pip warnings for yanked package notices
workarounds.how: pip warns on yanked installs since pip 20.0. CI should treat pip warnings as errors: pip install --strict

workarounds.action: For critical security issues, file a PyPI deletion request (not just yank)
workarounds.how: Contact PyPI support for complete deletion of malicious packages. Yank is insufficient for malware.
